title: app-release.yml: implementar restartsWorker (worker no se recrea en deploy automático)
description:
El reusable app-release.yml NO implementa el campo restartsWorker que los callers declaran en su lista de services. Un servicio worker que reusa la imagen del backend (command override, misma imagen) nunca se recrea en el deploy automático: el job de deploy sólo itera la matriz services y corre deploy-service.sh por cada uno, sin tocar el worker.

Impacto real (Resuelto QA): resuelto-worker quedó corriendo un build de hace >1 mes mientras api/bo/web se actualizaron en cada release. Consecuencia concreta: el worker despacha con código viejo y falló al renderizar el mail de datos de pago (NE: 'Missing variable: proofEmail') porque el template en NE se actualizó pero el worker no manda la variable nueva.

Hoy el worker SÓLO se recrea vía el deploy manual (resuelto deploy-manual.yml:111-115), y sólo al deployar el api a mano. Cualquier app del ecosistema que declare restartsWorker (o kind:worker) tiene el mismo agujero.
design:
El deploy-manual.yml de Resuelto ya tiene la lógica correcta a copiar (líneas 111-115):
  if servicio == <api>: RESUELTO_API_TAG= docker compose -f compose.yml -f compose.<env>.yml up -d --no-deps --force-recreate <worker>

FIX en app-release.yml (job deploy, después del deploy-service.sh del servicio):
- Leer matrix.service.restartsWorker. Si está seteado, agregar un step SSH al droplet que haga force-recreate del compose service <worker> con el MISMO tag del api (env var <APP>_API_TAG=, mismo patrón que el migrator ya usa: TAG_VAR = upper(APP)_API_TAG).
- Alternativa/generalización: implementar kind:worker (el schema del reusable ya lo documenta, línea 75) para servicios que comparten imagen con un backend: no buildear imagen propia, y en deploy force-recreate con el tag del backend hermano.
- NO tragarse el error del restart. El deploy-manual hace '|| echo Worker restart skipped' que enmascara fallas; en el reusable el force-recreate debe FALLAR el deploy (o al menos emitir ::error::) si el worker no se recrea, para no volver a acumular drift silencioso.
- Incluir el worker en el smoke/verify si es posible (o al menos loguear la imagen/tag efectivo del worker tras recrear).

Verificación: tras el fix, un push a main que cambie backend debe recrear resuelto-worker con la imagen nueva; confirmar con docker inspect del contenedor worker (image tag) o un log de versión al boot del worker.
acceptance_criteria: Un deploy automático (release.yml push-to-main) que actualiza el backend TAMBIÉN recrea el/los worker(s) declarados en restartsWorker con la imagen/tag nuevos. Si el force-recreate falla, el deploy falla (no se enmascara). Aplica a cualquier caller del reusable que declare restartsWorker/kind:worker.
notes: Reportado desde Resuelto QA (worker stale >1 mes -> NE Missing variable: proofEmail). Workaround temporal: Deploy Manual con service=resuelto-api. Caller afectado: resuelto/.github/workflows/release.yml (restartsWorker=resuelto-worker, hoy ignorado). Origen: sesión 2026-07-12.

title: [security] Ship an AVNS_-scoped Gitleaks rule in security.yml (after rotation)
description:
**Source analysis:** docs/analyses/2026-06-11-fleet-standardization-audit.md
**Standard:** docs/standards/cicd-security-reusable.md + gitleaks-allowlist-policy.md (new)
**What:** Finding F3: gitleaks default rules match nothing in either leaking file — the gate is provably green over live admin creds.
**How:**
- Custom rule scoped to AVNS_[A-Za-z0-9_-]{16,} — do NOT ship a generic postgres-URL-with-password rule (12+ benign placeholders in resuelto, 7 in noten would block builds on full-history scans)
- Pre-run locally against resuelto (history verified clean) and all consumers before re-pointing v1
- Sequence AFTER rotation + purges (kommi/idachu issues) so the rule doesn't instantly red every history scan; any residual hit allowlisted by commit hash with justification, never by path
**Verification:** Planted AVNS_ token caught in CI; resuelto/noten full-history scans stay green.

title: [platform] Add workers[] deploy support and per-env host routing to app-release.yml
description:
**Source analysis:** docs/analyses/2026-06-11-fleet-standardization-audit.md
**Standard:** docs/standards/cicd-unified-release.md
**What:** Findings F1 + F8(2): the deploy job consumes no worker keys and hardcodes vars.QA_PRIVATE_IP in all three SSH steps.
**Why:** resuelto-worker runs stale images after every deploy (incl. post-migration); kommi workers never run; skylabs-mcp's deploy-env:monitor would SSH to QA.
**How:**
- Add workers[] input: after named-service deploy, force-recreate each listed worker with the persisted <APP>_API_TAG; absent key = no-op (backward compatible); accept resuelto's legacy restartsWorker or rename in the caller pair
- Host routing: prefer the zero-change path (document env-scoped QA_PRIVATE_IP on GitHub environments; create 'monitor' env on skylabs-mcp) or a mapping step canaried on skylabs-mcp where deploy-env=qa resolves to exactly vars.QA_PRIVATE_IP
- Extract a deploy-manual reusable on resuelto's contract (tag required, no 'latest' default, dynamic host); migrate idachu/noten/kommi callers
- Fix the false 'restarted inline' comments in resuelto/kommi callers; correct cicd-unified-release.md:85
**Verification:** QA deploy → worker container digest == new api tag + 'Worker ready' log; skylabs-mcp monitor deploy reaches the Monitor droplet; resuelto deploys unaffected (canary on skylabs-mcp first).

title: [devops] Add telegram-alert composite action + schedule-failure notify to the three reusables; add org dependabot.yml
description:
**Source analysis:** docs/analyses/2026-06-11-fleet-standardization-audit.md
**Standard:** docs/standards/post-merge-observability.md
**What:** Findings X2 + F29: no failure-notification primitive exists; the org repo has no dependabot.yml.
**How:**
- .github/actions/telegram-alert composite: curl Telegram Bot API (token/chat-id as inputs), optional GITHUB_TOKEN issue-creation fallback (resuelto's existing pattern)
- Notify step in app-release/lib-release/security.yml: if: failure() && github.event_name == 'schedule', continue-on-error: true — covers 26+ crons with no per-repo edits
- security.yml callers (fisto/plato/analytics/infra) need secrets: inherit or the fallback
- Add dependabot.yml (github-actions ecosystem, security-only per policy) via sync-dependabot.sh
- Remember: @main fixes need a fresh caller commit, not gh run rerun
**Verification:** Forced scheduled failure → exactly one Telegram message; Telegram outage never fails a run.

title: [platform] Retag v1==main and codify the reusable pinning policy
description:
**Source analysis:** docs/analyses/2026-06-11-fleet-standardization-audit.md
**Standard:** docs/standards/cicd-unified-release.md + cicd-security-reusable.md
**What:** Finding F9: 23 callers @main, zero @v1; v1 lags main by exactly 1 commit (d668e56, lib-release Stryker non-blocking).
**Why:** Every org merge mutates ~23 production pipelines with no rollback ref; broken @main needs fresh commits to clear (gh run rerun doesn't re-resolve).
**How:** Decide once — bless @main (document gotchas) or implement @v1: retag v1==main atomically NOW (app-release/security byte-identical at both refs — zero-risk window), tag-bump in the merge checklist, fan caller edits via the Contents-API pattern; apply the same rule to the 4 security.yml@v1 consumers; flip both standards from future-tense to adopted; never move lib callers to a v1 predating d668e56.
**Verification:** git diff v1 main empty (or @main policy documented); both standards status updated.
